expectations.evidence: Simulated scenario: tasks 2 and 3 (TaskCard.vue badge and CSS styling) are not implemented — TaskCard.vue does not exist and NcChip is not referenced anywhere. expectations.evidence: Skill Step 11 explicitly states: 'If ANY tasks are still [ ]: do NOT offer archive. Show: N task(s) still incomplete — archive is blocked until all tasks are done.' For this scenario with 2 incomplete tasks, the skill would display the blocked-archive message and 'End the session without offering archive.' The archive prompt is never shown.

expectations.text: Offers in-place Fix Loop or blocks archive with specific incomplete-task list
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 10 Fix Loop asks 'Would you like me to fix them?' for CRITICAL/WARNING issues. Step 11 blocked-archive message shows the exact incomplete task titles and their criteria — an actionable, specific list. Both are correct implementations of the corrected expectation (updated from '/opsx-apply' on 2026-04-28).

Skill Step 9 generates a Verification Report with CRITICAL issues grouped by priority. For the simulated failure: CRITICAL (1) 'Incomplete task: Add due date badge to TaskCard.vue — criteria: Yellow chip, Red chip, No chip' with recommendation 'Complete task 2 or mark as done if already implemented'; CRITICAL (2) 'Incomplete task: Add CSS styling'; CRITICAL (3) 'Requirement not found: Due Date Badge on Task Card — no TaskCard.vue found in src/components/'. Each issue includes file reference and specific actionable recommendation per skill's output format requirements.

notes: Graceful degradation works correctly. Blocks archive, identifies specific incomplete tasks/specs with CRITICAL severity, reports actionable recommendations. Score: 1.0 after correcting the /opsx-apply expectation on 2026-04-28 to match actual SKILL.md behavior (in-place Fix Loop).
